{"key_event":"Non-Cooperation transforms nationalism into mass mobilization","reasoning":"The colonial state remained very strong: wartime emergency powers, Rowlatt repression, fiscal extraction, and post-1922 restoration of control preserved a pervasive coercive-administrative apparatus despite legitimacy losses. Civil society rose substantially as Gandhi-era satyagraha, Khilafat/Non-Cooperation, labor strikes, peasant mobilization, and new representative arenas expanded collective action, though fragmentation and the suspension of Non-Cooperation kept it below routinely constraining the state.","society_change":1.5,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.5}
